name: Chasca
description: Chasca, also known as Ch'aska or Qhasqa, is a significant deity in Incan mythology, representing the planet Venus as both the morning and evening star. She is often depicted as a young, beautiful maiden with long, flowing hair, symbolizing the dawn and the twilight. Chasca is associated with the transition between day and night, embodying the liminal spaces and times that hold particular power in Incan cosmology. Her role extends to protecting young girls, particularly those who are virgins, and she is considered a patron of those who tend to their hair. Some accounts link her to the concept of beauty and grace, reflecting the celestial splendor of Venus. While not as prominent as some other Incan deities like Inti (the sun god) or Mama Quilla (the moon goddess), Chasca holds a vital place in the Incan pantheon, symbolizing cyclical renewal and the ever-present beauty of the natural world. Offerings and prayers were sometimes directed to Chasca, especially by those seeking her protection or favor. She acts as a celestial guardian, watching over the world and ensuring the continuation of the cosmic order. The Incas, keen observers of the sky, revered Chasca for her consistent presence and the predictable nature of her appearances, which helped them track time and seasons. Her dual nature as both morning and evening star reinforces the Incan emphasis on duality and balance in the universe. Chasca's influence extends to themes of beauty, youth, and the cyclical nature of time, making her a compelling figure in Incan beliefs.
subtitle: The Venus Maiden of Twilight and Dawn

keyMyths.title: Chasca's Celestial Journey
keyMyths.description: Chasca travels across the sky as Venus, representing the transition between day and night. Her journey symbolizes the cyclical nature of time and the beauty of the cosmos.

keyMyths.title: Chasca and the Maidens
keyMyths.description: Chasca protects young maidens and is considered a patron of those who tend to their hair. She embodies youth, beauty, and grace.

keyMyths.title: The Morning and Evening Star
keyMyths.description: Chasca's appearance as both the morning and evening star highlights the Incan emphasis on duality and balance in the universe. This reinforces her role as a deity of transition.

sources.author: Bauer, Brian S.
sources.work: The Sacred Landscape of the Inca: The Cusco Region
sources.description: Discusses the Incan understanding of celestial bodies.
sources.text: Discusses the Incan understanding of celestial bodies.
